trace_table.claim: Page title, social title, and hero heading identify the About page for Women in Aviation Middle East with the headline 'A regional aviation community that opens doors.'
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About page heading

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME is the Middle East chapter of Women in Aviation International and connects women across aviation and aerospace roles in the region.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Who we are

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME's hub is Dubai, members live and work across more than forty countries, and the chapter is run by volunteers led by women in the industry.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Who we are; 01 Organization/01 Branding.md

trace_table.claim: The at-a-glance figures state 2013, 1,300+ members, 40+ countries, and 750+ attendees at the 2019 conference.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About story and open-items decisions; 03 Members/03 Member List Cleanup - Findings (2026-06-13).md

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME started in May 2013 at the Dubai Airport Show under the patronage of His Highness Sheikh Ahmed bin Saeed Al Maktoum, held its first board meeting that October, was recognised by WAI International in November 2014, and operates with The Emirates Aerosports Federation in Dubai.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Our story; 01 Organization/01 Legal Standing & Chapter Authority (Decision).md

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME has appeared at Dubai Airshow, Abu Dhabi Air Expo, MEBAA, the Airport Show in Dubai, the Global Aerospace Summit, and the Kuwait Aviation Show, and the 2019 annual conference drew more than 750 attendees, 26 international speakers, and a dozen sponsor companies.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Our story and open-item 10

trace_table.claim: The story says WAI-ME grew from founding members in 2013 to more than 1,300 members across the Middle East and beyond.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About member-count decision 2026-06-27

trace_table.claim: The mission lists four points about encouraging careers and education, giving resources and support, serving as a regional home, and bringing successful women forward as mentors and role models.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Our mission

trace_table.claim: The vision describes a region where women's contributions are celebrated, women are treated as equals, and women already inside aviation bring the next ones in.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Our vision, approved 2026-06-27

trace_table.claim: The language statement says WAI-ME welcomes in Arabic and equips in English because aviation works in English while Arabic is part of identity and front-door explanation.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About language block; 02 Platform/02 Language - English-First, Arabic Front Door (Decision).md

trace_table.claim: Leadership intro says WAI-ME is run by a volunteer team of women who have built aviation careers and now open doors for the next ones.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Leadership

trace_table.claim: The board roster names Ms. Mervat Sultan as President, Ms. Dina Bel Jaflah as Vice President, and Captain Kristina Tervo, Ms. Farah Al Ansari, Eng. Suaad Al Shamsi, and Ms. Heba Hussain as Board Members, with the listed LinkedIn links.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Leadership roster confirmed 2026-06-27

trace_table.claim: The Ambassadors block displays the public empty-state copy 'Coming soon'.
trace_table.vault_source: No vault note found. The governing About note instead says the Ambassadors section should show active members who help run the community, with photo and one line each, and that Mervat picks the first launch Ambassadors.

trace_table.claim: A full pilot licence was funded at the Riyadh General Assembly in December 2025 through a scholarship initiative worth more than USD 150,000, including a complete ATPL worth over USD 130,000 awarded to Rahaf Kaddoura.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Impact; 01 Organization/01.02 Membership Model/01.02 Preferred Flight Academy - IAA (Decision).md

trace_table.claim: The annual conference was co-located with the Airport Show in Dubai, held under the patronage of His Highness Sheikh Ahmed bin Saeed Al Maktoum, and reached more than 750 attendees, 26 international speakers, and a dozen sponsors by 2019.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Impact and open-item 10

trace_table.claim: The community spans more than 1,300 members across more than forty countries, with most in their twenties and thirties.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Impact; 01.02 Individual Membership - Decision & Handoff.md

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME has been present at major regional aviation events every year since 2013, from Dubai and Abu Dhabi to Kuwait and Riyadh.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About Impact

trace_table.claim: Partnerships with regional flight academies give members a route to discounted and funded training, kept plural and unnamed.

trace_table.claim: Closing CTA says there is room for the reader, membership is free, and companies can back the next generation as corporate partners.

orphan_claims: The Ambassadors section displays 'Coming soon' with no vault-approved public copy, and the governing vault note instead specifies an Ambassadors section made of active members with photo and one line each.
required_fixes: Replace the Ambassadors empty state with vault-sourced launch Ambassadors after Mervat records the selected names, photos, and one-line descriptions in the vault, or remove the block until the vault supplies approved public empty-state copy.
summary: FAIL: the About page's main factual copy and brand locks trace to the vault, but the public Ambassadors empty-state copy has no vault source. Gate 4 cannot pass while that orphan public claim remains.
